summary: Soundboard capture of an electrifying 8/4/89 Cal Expo run, highlighted by a ripping Bertha opener, Brent’s wild Queen Jane piano solo and a beloved Stella Blue encore.
review: The recording is a clean sound‑board source that delivers clear instrument separation and plenty of stage energy.  Fans rave about the long, high‑energy first set (Bertha, Althea, Built to Last) and a second half packed with Queen Jane, Cumberland, Crazy Fingers and a soaring Stella Blue encore.  Overall the show is praised for its tight playing, Jerry’s birthday tribute in Space and Brent’s standout piano work.

ai_rating.rationale: 22 reviews with an average of 4.45 stars; overwhelmingly positive comments about both performance and sound quality, only a single 3‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as clear and well balanced; no major dropouts or muddiness noted. Listeners mention “nice sound”, “energy and sound clarity was awesome” and “sound is good”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans highlight an unusually long, 11‑song first set that kept the energy high, followed by a second set that opened with Truckin' and delivered strong mid‑set peaks (Queen Jane piano solo, Cumberland) before closing on a beloved Stella Blue encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his high energy, vocal performance and birthday tribute during Space; several reviewers note his guitar tone and overall presence as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s piano work is a focal point – many call it “wild”, “awesome” and a standout solo on Queen Jane (though one reviewer joked it ‘hurt’ listeners).
